heading: Job Corps oversight and reporting
label: 3211 Job Corps oversight and reporting
notes: Effective DateSection effective on the first day of the first full program year after (), see , set out as a note under .July 22, 2014July 1, 2015section 506 of Pub. L. 113–128section 3101 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: information regarding the implementation of the financial oversight measures suggested in the , report of the Office of Inspector General of the Department of Labor entitled “The U.S. Department of Labor’s Employment and Training Administration Needs to Strengthen Controls over Job Corps Funds”;May 31, 2013

children.label: B
children.text: a description of any budgetary shortfalls for the program for the period covered by the financial report, and the reasons for such shortfalls; and

children.label: C
children.text: a description and explanation for any approval for contract expenditures that are in excess of the amounts provided for under the contract.

children.heading: Timing of reports
children.label: 2 Timing of reports
children.text: The Secretary shall submit a financial report under paragraph (1) once every 6 months beginning on , for a 3-year period. After the completion of such 3-year period, the Secretary shall submit a financial report under such paragraph once a year for the next 2 years, unless additional reports are required under paragraph (3)(B).July 22, 2014

children.heading: Reporting requirements in cases of budgetary shortfalls
children.label: 3 Reporting requirements in cases of budgetary shortfalls
A
children.text: not later than 90 days after the budgetary shortfall was identified, submit a report to the applicable committees explaining how the budgetary shortfall will be addressed; and

children.label: B
children.text: submit an additional financial report under paragraph (1) for each 6-month period subsequent to the finding of the budgetary shortfall until the Secretary demonstrates, through such report, that the Job Corps program has no budgetary shortfall.

children.heading: Third-party review
children.label: b Third-party review
children.text: Every 5 years after , the Secretary shall provide for a third-party review of the Job Corps program under this part that addresses all of the areas described in subparagraphs (A) through (G) of . The results of the review shall be submitted to the Committee on Education and the Workforce of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate.July 22, 2014section 3224(a)(2) of this title

children.heading: Criteria for Job Corps center closures
children.label: c Criteria for Job Corps center closures
children.text: By not later than , the Secretary shall establish written criteria that the Secretary shall use to determine when a Job Corps center supported under this part is to be closed and how to carry out such closure, and shall submit such criteria to the applicable committees.December 1, 2014
the Committee on Education and the Workforce of the House of Representatives;
the Subcommittee on Labor, Health and Human Services, Education, and Related Agencies of the Committee of Appropriations of the House of Representatives;
the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate; and
the Subcommittee on Labor, Health and Human Services, Education, and Related Agencies of the Committee of Appropriations of the Senate.
